channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.description:
Outdated council notice on lampost
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Darwin Road, Falconwood, Greater London

item.title: Garbage piling up, 19th May
item.description:
It hasn’t cleared from a month at this point….

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Church Street, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Littered street, 19th May
item.description:
Littered street

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41, Stockwell Green, Stockwell, Greater London

item.title: Baby chair, 19th May
item.description:
Baby chair

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41, Stockwell Green, Stockwell, Greater London

item.title: Serious hygiene issue caused by uncontrolled rubbish dumping, 19th May
item.description:
The rubbish stored here is creating a serious hygiene issue, rats and cockroaches, leaking cooking fat dumped. All sort of rubbish dumbed uncontrolled here.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsland High Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bags of curtains, mirro and vacuum dumped, 19th May
item.description:
Flytipping of unwanted household waste

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Elfindale Road, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Completely blocked road drain on Gertrude Road, 19th May
item.description:
This drain has been blocked since Thames water left. Reported previously. Someone came and tried to put a spade down it -has made no difference

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gertrude Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uneven road surface causes car to shake so violently that I’m worried for my newborns safety despite being in car seat, 19th May
item.description:
The whole area under the yellow box junction between iron mill lane and Perry street is really uneven. There is no route you can take that doesn’t shake the car quite violently. There doesn’t seem to be any visible damage to the road it is just vastly uneven and needs resurfacing.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Street, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree branch interferring with telegraph pole and cables, 19th May
item.description:
Branches causing constant interferrence with signal. 5 weeks now and getting worse. Tree is in kentwood campus opposite 11 kingsdale road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kingsdale Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pot hole near Hilldrop road, 19th May
item.description:
There are three pot holes at the crossing between Hilldrop and Brecknock Road. Location is on Brecknock Road near the bus stop but right in the middle of the street. I have slipped on the bike there almost three times.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 87, Brecknock Road, Tufnell Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter in St Mellion Close, 19th May
item.description:
Please, who is responsible for cleaning the end of St Mellion Close? There is constantly litter.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Mellion Close, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sign is on the pavement, 19th May
item.description:
The sign is on the floor for a few weeks now.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Craven Park Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping outside No 1 St Martins Close DA184DZ, 19th May
item.description:
Fly tipping outside No 1 St Martins Close DA184DZ. Occupants have left and fly tipped their rubbish. Please clear ASAP as history shows leeaving too long encourages others to dump additional items with it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Martins Close, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped Lime Bike, 19th May
item.description:
Dumped Lime bike on pedestrian footway. I do not believe that Greenwich Council operate Lime bikes in their borough

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eltham Hill, Well Hall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping, 19th May
item.description:
Please put a warning sign

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Timberwharf Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of rubbish and broken furniture at the end of Rye Hill Park, 19th May
item.description:
Once again furniture has been dumped at the end of the street as well as bags of rubbish that have split and blown all across the road as well as into people's properties. There are children that live in these houses and it's a big concern for their safety and health.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rye Hill Park, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Repeated fly tipping, 19th May
item.description:
Yet more fly tipping. This is happening on a weekly basis. Taxpayers money being used to clear away rubbish dumped by lazy people. There appears to be no preventative measures put in place to prevent this from happening.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pontefract Road, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole, 19th May
item.description:
There is a pothole in the road surface, disrupting the white keep clear markings.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Black Prince Road, Vauxhall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish everywhere, 19th May
item.description:
This place is a dumb. It's hardly ever clean when I walk past it going towards the park. The bins are over flowing and it seems residents just dump all there stuff behind a low wall. A lot of the rubbish is slung across the pavement and road. The pictures I have added, one is from 2016 and one is from 19/05/2026, so yes 10 years of this no doubt. It's a shame as it's a nice road and yet when you have an estate, which is partly council it's a sh*thole, who would have thought it? (you'll probably cry as you can't take criticism) If this is run by phoenix it wouldn't surprise me. I will keep reporting this until the problem is resolved. It's the caretakers duty to sort out the bins and, get bigger bins if the residents are over filling the ones they have. I live in a phoenix property and we have a communal bin, Lewishman council special edition & it supports 6 properties no problem. I would not be surprised if a lot of the stuff dumped behind that low wall is actually not from residents but just the general public who saw residents use it and thought they could abuse the situation.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravensbourne Avenue, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
